event_description: A large apron object is matched across EO and IR using stand fixtures, oval pad geometry, and nearby aircraft/service-object distractors.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, or rationales to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v13: retained from the harder Air_Canada set because its smoke score is already near the target range.
acceptance_gates: Run prompt leakage audit after request export.
Correct options must not be systematically longer or more specific than distractors.
Avoid class-name-only answers when a relational visual description can be used.
Questions should be answerable by a human viewer without hidden annotation metadata.

qa.question: In the EO apron view, which orientation plus stand relation fits the large object?
In the close IR stand view, which pole relation is visible around the long dark body?

options.D: no vertical pole overlaps the body

qa.question: In the wider IR view of several stands, which neighborhood matches the target stand?

options.A: oval pads, service lanes, parked bodies
options.B: straight runway strip with no oval pads nearby
options.C: terminal gate row with roof covering bodies
options.D: grass infield with a single isolated body

qa.question: Which EO relation argues that the object is parked at a stand rather than already rolling on a runway?

options.A: stand fixtures sit by body on apron
options.B: centerline markings run directly under the body
options.C: the body sits inside grass with no pavement pads
options.D: the nose crosses a runway threshold marking

qa.question: Across the IR views, which relation stays stable even as the camera scale changes?

options.A: long dark body tied to oval stand pad
options.B: two bright dots leave the pad as a moving pair
options.C: small vehicle blob becomes the main long body
options.D: tail-side heat trail grows into the whole object

qa.question: Which EO detail makes the taxiway interpretation less direct?

options.A: stand equipment sits beside the body
options.B: runway threshold bars sit directly under the body
options.C: grass fills the full area under both wings
options.D: a long road lane separates the object from the apron

qa.question: Which EO/IR pair points to the same stand occupant rather than a neighboring stand?

options.A: EO mid-body pole; IR long body on one pad
options.B: EO nose-side pole; IR long body on next pad
options.C: EO tail-side pole; IR long body on taxi lane
options.D: EO no nearby pole; IR long body over grass

qa.question: Which paired observation best rejects a tail-plume or service-vehicle reading?

options.A: EO stand layout; IR broad body on pad
options.B: EO runway strip; IR thin tail-side streak
options.C: EO stand layout; IR small nose-side blob
options.D: EO grass strip; IR two compact hot points

qa.question: Which EO evidence chain should be used before choosing among neighboring stands?

options.B: grass strip below body -> rural grass patch later
options.C: terminal roof line -> hangar wall beside body
options.D: runway threshold -> centerline under the nose

qa.question: Which IR evidence separates the target stand occupant from smaller apron shapes?

options.B: two compact dots crossing between pads
options.C: small blob parked at the nose-side edge
options.D: thin tail-side streak with no broad body

qa.question: Which cross-modal chain follows the parked object through viewpoint change?

options.A: EO mid-body pole -> IR pad body -> EO apron pads
options.B: EO nose-side pole -> IR pad body -> EO apron pads
options.C: EO tail-side pole -> IR pad body -> EO apron pads
options.D: EO runway stripe -> IR pad body -> EO apron pads

qa.question: Which final chain uses concrete cues from both videos without switching stands?

options.A: mid-body pole, oval pad, broad dark body
options.B: nose-side pole, oval pad, broad dark body
options.C: tail-side pole, oval pad, broad dark body
options.D: runway stripe, oval pad, broad dark body
